Failure Description: I studied well for the exam but when I saw the question paper, I got nervous and forgot everything.
Reflection: I realized that preparation alone is not enough; I must also train myself to handle pressure. I need to practice under exam-like conditions and build confidence through repetition and exposure.

Failure Description: I studied all the topics and felt confident, but when solving questions I struggled because I didn’t fully understand the concepts. My preparation was more focused on memorization than deep understanding.
Reflection: I learned that true understanding is more important than memorization. I need to focus on conceptual clarity, problem-solving, and applying knowledge rather than just reading theory.
